truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 against the provided ground truth. 6 vulnerabilities were found fully or partially, 14 were missed. 1 finding was admitted as unsupported by the ground truth scope. 1 CWE classification was corrected.

Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string vulnerability in log_debug_message and quoted the exact unsafe printf call.
Run 2 did not analyze the factorial lambda in FunctionRegistry for integer overflow on large inputs.
Run 2 failed to identify the use-after-free risk where scratch memory is freed by g_memory_pool.cleanup() while still referenced by a deferred thread pool task.
Run 2 correctly identified command injection in ConfigLoader::validate_config and quoted the system() call.
Run 2 correctly identified the inverted length check leading to stack buffer overflow and quoted the exact conditional.
Run 2 identified a race condition in the getter but focused on the volatile read rather than the critical race in increment() where old_value is read outside the lock.
Run 2 did not flag the EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE logic bomb that bypasses authentication after 5 attempts.
Run 2 did not identify the path traversal risk in load_config where filename is concatenated directly to config_dir_ without validation.
Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow in the expression parser due to unbounded strcpy into a 256-byte buffer.
Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded administrator secret and quoted the exact definition.
Run 2 did not identify the weak PRNG (mt19937 with small range) used for session token generation.
Run 2 did not flag the thread-unsafe static cached_time variable updated via localtime() in log_debug_message.
Run 2 did not identify the insecure temporary file handling and command injection risk in append_override_audit_event.
Run 2 did not flag the persistent authentication state that remains true across sessions without expiration or invalidation.
Run 2 did not identify the unhandled std::stod exception in handle_variable_command when tokens[3] is not a valid number.
Run 2 did not analyze the memory pool split logic for integer underflow when previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES wraps.
Run 2 did not identify the unbounded recursion risk in parse_factor/parse_expression with deeply nested parentheses.
Run 2 did not flag the data race in evaluate_cached where result_cache_ is accessed concurrently without consistent mutex protection across enqueue calls.
Run 2 did not identify the TOCTOU race between std::filesystem::exists() and std::ifstream() due to the sleep_for delay.
Run 2 did not flag the integer division by zero in calculate_route_score when token_length is 0.

Corrections.PreviousClaim: "Heap Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser" classified as CWE-120
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: "Heap Overflow in Expression Parser" correctly classified as CWE-122
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: command injection via admin console|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|620|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated users can execute arbitrary operating system commands with the privileges of the running process, leading to full system compromise.|an authenticated admin submits a command starting with 'exec ' followed by a malicious shell command.|remove use of system(). implement a strict allowlist of permitted commands or use safe execution apis with explicit argument parsing and shell escaping.->command injection via admin console|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|620|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated users can execute arbitrary operating system commands with the privileges of the running process, leading to full system compromise.|an authenticated admin submits a command starting with 'exec ' followed by a malicious shell command.|remove use of system(). implement a strict allowlist of permitted commands or use safe execution apis with explicit argument parsing and shell escaping.

stack buffer overflow due to inverted length check|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|inputs >= 1024 bytes trigger an unbounded strcpy into a fixed 1024-byte stack buffer, causing stack corruption and potential code execution.|user inputs a calculation string with length greater than or equal to 1024 characters.|invert the condition to `if (expression.length() < sizeof(input_buffer_))` or use a safe bounded copy function.->stack buffer overflow due to inverted length check|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }|inputs >= 1024 bytes trigger an unbounded strcpy into a fixed 1024-byte stack buffer, causing stack corruption and potential code execution.|user inputs a calculation string with length greater than or equal to 1024 characters.|invert the condition to `if (expression.length() < sizeof(input_buffer_))` or use a safe bounded copy function.
